questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: Under the framework of the Biological Weapons Convention (BWC), which of the following are categorized as biological weapons or agents?

options.text: Bacteria, Viruses, Fungi, and Prions only
Bacteria, Viruses, Fungi, Prions, and Toxins

questions.explanation: The BWC covers a broad range of biological agents. According to the definition, it includes disease-causing organisms such as bacteria, viruses, fungi, prions, or rickettsiae. It also covers toxins, which are poisons derived from animals, plants, or microorganisms.
questions.takeaway: The BWC definition of biological weapons includes both living organisms (like bacteria/fungi) and non-living poisonous substances (toxins).
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Biological Weapons: Weapons that disseminate disease-causing organisms (bacteria, viruses, fungi, prions or rickettsiae) or toxins (poisons derived from animals, plants or microorganisms) worldwide to harm or kill humans, animals or plants.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: With reference to Project Kusha, which was recently in the news, which of the following statements is correct?

options.text: It is a joint venture between India and Russia to upgrade the S-400 missile system.
It is an indigenous initiative led by DRDO to develop a long-range air defense missile system.
It is a cyber-security mission aimed at protecting India's railway and hospital infrastructure.
It is a naval mission focused on securing the Indian Ocean Region using indigenous carrier-based aircraft.

questions.explanation: Project Kusha is an indigenous initiative led by the Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) to develop a long-range air defense missile system (LR-SAM). The Indian Air Force is currently pressing for its rapid progress as part of India's broader air defense strategy.
questions.takeaway: Project Kusha is the specific DRDO-led project for long-range air defense, distinct from the broader Sudarshan Chakra Mission umbrella.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Additionally, the Indian Air Force is pressing for rapid progress on Project Kusha, an indigenous initiative led by DRDO to develop a long-range air defense missile system.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: Which of the following best describes the integration strategy of the Sudarshan Chakra Mission?

options.text: It focuses exclusively on physical infrastructure safeguards to prevent kinetic attacks on strategic sites.
It combines advanced surveillance, cyber protection, and physical infrastructure safeguards into a unified system.
It relies on a decentralized model where each state develops its own regional security shield by 2035.
It prioritizes offensive cyber capabilities over defensive physical missile shields to ensure deterrence.

questions.explanation: The Sudarshan Chakra Mission is characterized by its integrated approach. It does not just focus on physical missiles; it integrates advanced surveillance, cyber protection, and physical infrastructure safeguards to create a comprehensive national security shield.
questions.takeaway: Sudarshan Chakra is a multi-domain mission (cyber, surveillance, physical) rather than a single-technology project.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: The mission will be integrating advanced surveillance, cyber protection, and physical infrastructure safeguards. It will be a system for targeted precise action.
